description_long: This object is a standard wooden pallet, constructed from several parallel planks of weathered wood, spaced evenly apart on the top surface. The pallet features three thick wooden beams running perpendicular underneath, providing structural support and creating two large open slots for forklift or pallet jack access. The wood appears aged and rough, with visible grain, knots, and some discoloration, suggesting outdoor or industrial use. The sides are open, allowing for easy lifting and stacking, while the overall form is flat and rectangular. There are no visible fasteners, handles, or closures, and the design is purely functional for supporting and transporting goods. The pallet is utilitarian, commonly found in warehouses, loading docks, and urban storage areas.
description: A weathered wooden pallet with spaced planks and open slots for lifting, designed for supporting and transporting goods. Its rough, utilitarian appearance suggests industrial or outdoor use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat surface of the pallet with parallel wooden planks and two large open slots beneath.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the spacing between the top planks and the three supporting beams underneath.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the same open slots and plank arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring the first side, with visible gaps between planks and supporting beams.
attribute_placement_location: floor, warehouse, loading dock, outdoor, stackable
